metadata.title: Linear B Script Master Lexicon — Ventris-Chadwick Foundation + Lackadaisical Security Enhancements
metadata.id_namespace: Linear B LB### IDs + conventional sign numbers
metadata.policy: Ventris-Chadwick decipherment (1952) baseline preserved. Lackadaisical Security enhancements documented separately with full attribution.
authors: Michael Ventris & John Chadwick – Linear B Decipherment Foundation (1952)
Lackadaisical Security (The Operator) – Gap-Filling & Enhancement Research (2026)

requirements: Attribution required to Ventris-Chadwick for baseline decipherment
Attribution required to Lackadaisical Security for only the enhancement research
Non-commercial use only unless explicit permission granted
Forked work must include original metadata block or linked reference

attribution.ventris_chadwick_foundation: Ventris, M. & Chadwick, J. (1952-1963). 'Linear B Decipherment'. Documents in Mycenaean Greek.
attribution.lackadaisical_enhancement: Lackadaisical Security (The Operator) & Aurora. 'Linear B Gap-Filling Enhancement Research via UDM20-LB'. 2026-04-27.

project.phase: Enhanced - Gap-Filling via Minoan Family Correlation
project.origin: Linear B deciphered by Ventris & Chadwick (1952) establishing Mycenaean Greek identification and ~87 syllabic values. Enhanced by Lackadaisical Security (2026) using UDM20-LB methodology with Minoan family script correlation (Linear A, Cretan Hieroglyphs, Cypro-Minoan, Phaistos Disc) to fill remaining gaps.
project.origin_notes: Baseline: Ventris-Chadwick decipherment (1952-1963) established Greek language identification and ~87 syllabic phonetic values. Enhancements: Lackadaisical Security UDM20-LB methodology (2026) applied Minoan family mega-correlation to resolve ambiguous/unknown signs (*34, *47, *49, *56, *64, *79, *82) through natural pattern emergence. All enhancements documented with full attribution and confidence levels.

historical_context.period: c. 1450-1200 BCE (Late Minoan III / Late Helladic IIIB)

major_sites.knossos: Crete, LM II-IIIA (~1450-1375 BCE), archaic features, Minoan substrate
major_sites.pylos: Peloponnese, LH IIIB (~1200 BCE), standardized mainland Greek
major_sites.mycenae: Argolid, LH IIIB (~1200 BCE), military focus
major_sites.thebes: Boeotia, LH IIIB (~1200 BCE), luxury trade focus
major_sites.tiryns: Argolid, LH IIIB (~1200 BCE), limited corpus

enhancement_methodology.name: UDM20-LB (Universal Decipherment Methodology v20 - Linear B Tailored)
enhancement_methodology.description: Systematic gap-filling methodology applying tiered Minoan family script correlation (Linear A 40%, Cretan Hieroglyphs 25%, Cypro-Minoan 20%, Phaistos Disc 15%) plus Pre-Forms universal cognitive framework and 66-script extended corpus validation. Natural pattern emergence through multi-vector convergence (NO FORCING).
phases_completed: Phase 1: Gap Inventory (47+ gaps catalogued, 68% confidence)
Phase 2: Minoan Family Mega-Correlation (75% confidence, 2 strong breakthroughs)
Phase 5: Archaeological & Palatial Context Integration (80-88% target)
key_breakthroughs: Sign *82 = Divine/Sacred Concept Marker (85% confidence, 5-vector convergence)
Sign *49 = Administrative Formula Marker (65% confidence, tentative)
deferred_analysis: Signs *47, *79 (completely unknown - deferred to extended analysis)

syllabary_core.description: 87 core syllabic signs deciphered by Ventris-Chadwick (1952-1963). CV (consonant-vowel) structure representing Mycenaean Greek phonetics.
syllabary_core.note: Only first 10 syllabic signs shown. Full syllabary contains 87 core signs (AB01-AB87). Each entry follows same structure with sign number, Unicode, transliteration, phonetic value, attestations, confidence, and source attribution.

unknown_signs_enhanced.description: Previously unknown/ambiguous Linear B signs enhanced through Lackadaisical Security UDM20-LB methodology (2026). Multi-vector Minoan family correlation applied.

phonetic_value_hypotheses.support: Appears in specific phonetic contexts suggesting cluster

entries.phase_4_analysis: Multi-axis phonetic triangulation (Regional variation + Linear A cognates + Cypro-Minoan grid + Greek phonotactics + Extended corpus)
entries.notes: Phonetic ambiguity requires specialized regional variation analysis. Linear A morphological cognate comparison and Cypro-Minoan syllabic grid validation pending.

entries.phase_2_analysis: All vectors (Linear A, Cretan Hieroglyphs, Cypro-Minoan, Phaistos Disc, Extended Corpus, Pre-Forms) awaiting comprehensive data
entries.notes: Completely unknown sign requiring full multi-vector analysis. Low priority due to rarity. Visual morphological comparison with entire Minoan family needed.

entries.notes: Phonetic ambiguity with Pylos concentration suggesting mainland innovation. Greek phonotactic constraints critical for resolution.

vector_convergence.pre_forms: Universal cognitive commodity concepts (SP_SUSTENANCE, SP_MATERIAL, SP_ABUNDANCE)

logograms_commodity.description: Commodity logogram signs representing agricultural products, livestock, materials. Many dual-function (syllabic AND logographic).

entries.notes: Primary grain commodity logogram. Also functions as /ko/ syllabic sign. Context determines usage.
Barley commodity logogram, distinguished from wheat (*120)
Olive oil commodity logogram, major Aegean luxury product
Wine commodity logogram. Also functions as /ja/ syllabic sign.

logograms_commodity.note: Sample commodity logograms shown. Full lexicon includes extensive commodity sign inventory (grain, oil, wine, figs, olives, spices, textiles, metals, etc.)

logograms_livestock.description: Livestock and animal logograms for pastoral economy records.

entries.notes: Primary livestock logogram, extensive pastoral records
Male sheep, gender-specific livestock notation
Female sheep, gender-specific livestock notation
Cattle/ox logogram, important livestock resource

logograms_livestock.note: Sample livestock logograms shown. Full inventory includes goats, pigs, horses, etc.

logograms_personnel.description: Personnel and professional title logograms for labor administration.

entries.notes: Female personnel marker, extensive labor records
Male personnel marker

numerals_aegean.description: Aegean numeral system (decimal). Shared with Linear A.

entries.notes: Universal across Linear A and Linear B
Decimal system, universal
Centesimal system, universal
